top_data.title: Prompt fix: 5 badges consistently missed — add positive examples
top_data.evidence_summary: Production awards these but DaaS misses them: 'rally master' (6 missed), 'new look' (5 missed), 'steady eddie' (4 missed), 'paddle tap celebration' (3 missed), 'fresh fit' (3 missed). These are real badges that our prompt doesn't detect.
top_data.recommended_action:
Add positive examples to Gemini prompt:
'When analyzing badges, make sure to check for these commonly missed badges:
  - rally master: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - new look: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - steady eddie: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - paddle tap celebration: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - fresh fit: Look for [describe visual pattern]

follow_up.follow_up_question: Can we A/B test this on 50 clips?
follow_up.proposed_action:
Add positive examples to Gemini prompt:
'When analyzing badges, make sure to check for these commonly missed badges:
  - rally master: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - new look: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - steady eddie: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - paddle tap celebration: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - fresh fit: Look for [describe visual pattern]

top_data.title: DaaS badge prediction: F1=0.99 (precision=0.99, recall=0.98)
top_data.evidence_summary: Across 496 verified clips: 1924 correct predictions, 16 false positives, 41 missed badges. Overall F1=0.985.
top_data.recommended_action: Target recall improvement: add more badge examples to Gemini prompt

follow_up.follow_up_question: What's the ROI of fixing this? How many clips affected?
follow_up.proposed_action: Target recall improvement: add more badge examples to Gemini prompt

top_data.title: Worst predicted badges: third shot drop artist (F1=0.962), hustler (F1=0.923)
top_data.evidence_summary: Lowest F1 badges: third shot drop artist (F1=0.962), hustler (F1=0.923). These need prompt improvement.
top_data.recommended_action: Add specific examples for these badge types in Gemini prompt

follow_up.follow_up_question: What's the ROI of fixing this? How many clips affected?
follow_up.proposed_action: Add specific examples for these badge types in Gemini prompt

top_data.title: Prompt fix: JOOLA appears in 365/496 verified clips — add disambiguation
top_data.evidence_summary: JOOLA dominates 74% of clips. Prompt should distinguish: paddle logo (real JOOLA use), net logo (JOOLA sponsors nets), and venue branding (JOOLA sponsors the facility).
top_data.recommended_action:
Add to brand detection prompt:
'For JOOLA specifically, classify detection source:
  - paddle: JOOLA logo on paddle face = confirmed product use
  - net: JOOLA logo on net = venue sponsorship, NOT product use
  - venue: JOOLA on walls/banners = facility sponsorship
Only count paddle detections as brand_category: paddle'

follow_up.follow_up_question: Can we A/B test this on 50 clips?
follow_up.proposed_action:
Add to brand detection prompt:
'For JOOLA specifically, classify detection source:
  - paddle: JOOLA logo on paddle face = confirmed product use
  - net: JOOLA logo on net = venue sponsorship, NOT product use
  - venue: JOOLA on walls/banners = facility sponsorship
Only count paddle detections as brand_category: paddle'

top_20.title: Sell the Idea Lab itself — every facility gets their own AI research team
top_20.evidence_summary: The Idea Lab is currently an internal tool for us. But imagine every Courtana facility owner getting their own instance: '7 AI researchers analyzed YOUR 200 clips overnight. Top finding: your Tuesday evening players are 2x more likely to use premium paddles.' The facility just clicks approve/dismiss. They're not buying data — they're buying decisions.

top_20.title: Cross-corpus competitive intelligence — analyze competitor public content
top_20.evidence_summary: Scrape MLP/PPA YouTube highlights, run the same Gemini analysis, compare. 'Here is what the pros use vs what your recreational players use.' That cross-corpus comparison is something nobody else can do because nobody else has the pipeline.
top_20.recommended_action: Evaluate: competitive_intel play

top_20.title: Net/venue branding inflates 7 brands — true share-of-court differs from raw counts
top_20.evidence_summary: Brands with significant venue-only detections (not product use): JOOLA (497/6373 are venue), Nicol (6/14 are venue), PUSHIT (5/9 are venue), adidas (4/47 are venue), Onix (3/42 are venue). Raw counts overstate market share. Product-use only: JOOLA=5876, Nike=643, PICKLEBALL=201, Reolink=128, HEAD=113.
top_20.recommended_action: Report TRUE share-of-court (product-use only) to brand partners, not raw detection counts

top_20.title: Viral formula: quality (7.41 vs 4.51) + watchability (6.79 vs 2.5)
top_20.evidence_summary: Top 100 viral clips: quality=7.41, watchability=6.79. Bottom 100: quality=4.51, watchability=2.5. Both metrics track strongly with viral potential.
top_20.recommended_action: Use (quality + watchability) / 2 as quick viral prediction score

top_20.title: Player equipment tagging — let players tag their own gear for rewards
top_20.evidence_summary: Build a 'What's in my bag?' feature. Players tag their paddle, shoes, etc. after each session. Reward with badges/stats. This crowd-sources brand detection AND creates engagement loop.
top_20.recommended_action: Evaluate: crowdsource play

top_20.title: Reverse data marketplace — brands submit product data TO us
top_20.evidence_summary: Instead of detecting brands, invite them to our platform. They upload their product catalog, we match it against detections. They pay for the matching service. Flips the model entirely.
top_20.recommended_action: Evaluate: partnership_flip play

top_20.title: Rally length matters: long rallies are more viral (long=3.1 vs short=2.2)
top_20.evidence_summary: Avg rally: 15.6 shots. Long rallies (15+): 1974 clips, avg viral 3.1. Short (4-): 63 clips, avg viral 2.2.
top_20.recommended_action: Use rally length as a quality signal in highlight curation

top_20.title: Brand detection-as-a-service API — let anyone analyze sports footage
top_20.evidence_summary: Our Gemini pipeline works on any sports video. Open an API endpoint: upload video, get brand/skill JSON back. First customer = JOOLA. Second = their competitors.
top_20.recommended_action: Evaluate: platform_play play

top_20.title: Top content creators: PickleBill leads with 597 clips
top_20.evidence_summary:
PickleBill: 597 clips, viral=3.2, Steady Eddie
Chintan: 186 clips, viral=3.4, Dink Specialist
Coach_Block: 110 clips, viral=2.8, Steady Eddie
Tmf: 108 clips, viral=3.4, Steady Eddie
Ironvarr: 107 clips, viral=3.4, Kitchen Master
top_20.recommended_action: Create player spotlight feature for top contributors

source_clips.summary: Players engage in a consistent rally of dinks at the kitchen line.

Two players engage in a consistent, extended dink rally at the kitchen line.

top_20.title: Viral arc: 'athletic' is 220.0x more likely in top clips (22.0% vs 0.0%)
top_20.evidence_summary: Story arc 'athletic' appears in 22.0% of top-viral clips but only 0.0% of bottom-viral clips.
top_20.recommended_action: Prioritize 'athletic' clips in automated highlight reels

all_findings.title: Viral formula: quality (7.41 vs 4.51) + watchability (6.79 vs 2.5)
all_findings.evidence_summary: Top 100 viral clips: quality=7.41, watchability=6.79. Bottom 100: quality=4.51, watchability=2.5. Both metrics track strongly with viral potential.
all_findings.recommended_action: Use (quality + watchability) / 2 as quick viral prediction score

all_findings.title: Player equipment tagging — let players tag their own gear for rewards
all_findings.evidence_summary: Build a 'What's in my bag?' feature. Players tag their paddle, shoes, etc. after each session. Reward with badges/stats. This crowd-sources brand detection AND creates engagement loop.
all_findings.recommended_action: Evaluate: crowdsource play

all_findings.title: Reverse data marketplace — brands submit product data TO us
all_findings.evidence_summary: Instead of detecting brands, invite them to our platform. They upload their product catalog, we match it against detections. They pay for the matching service. Flips the model entirely.
all_findings.recommended_action: Evaluate: partnership_flip play

all_findings.title: Rally length matters: long rallies are more viral (long=3.1 vs short=2.2)
all_findings.evidence_summary: Avg rally: 15.6 shots. Long rallies (15+): 1974 clips, avg viral 3.1. Short (4-): 63 clips, avg viral 2.2.
all_findings.recommended_action: Use rally length as a quality signal in highlight curation

all_findings.title: Brand detection-as-a-service API — let anyone analyze sports footage
all_findings.evidence_summary: Our Gemini pipeline works on any sports video. Open an API endpoint: upload video, get brand/skill JSON back. First customer = JOOLA. Second = their competitors.
all_findings.recommended_action: Evaluate: platform_play play

all_findings.title: Top content creators: PickleBill leads with 597 clips
all_findings.evidence_summary:
PickleBill: 597 clips, viral=3.2, Steady Eddie
Chintan: 186 clips, viral=3.4, Dink Specialist
Coach_Block: 110 clips, viral=2.8, Steady Eddie
Tmf: 108 clips, viral=3.4, Steady Eddie
Ironvarr: 107 clips, viral=3.4, Kitchen Master
all_findings.recommended_action: Create player spotlight feature for top contributors

source_clips.summary: Players engage in a consistent rally of dinks at the kitchen line.
Two players engage in a consistent dink rally at the kitchen line.
Two players engage in a consistent, extended dink rally at the kitchen line.

all_findings.title: Viral arc: 'athletic' is 220.0x more likely in top clips (22.0% vs 0.0%)
all_findings.evidence_summary: Story arc 'athletic' appears in 22.0% of top-viral clips but only 0.0% of bottom-viral clips.
all_findings.recommended_action: Prioritize 'athletic' clips in automated highlight reels

all_findings.title: Coaching drill marketplace — sell AI-generated drills to players directly
all_findings.evidence_summary: Every clip generates coaching insights. Package these as personalized drills. Charge $5/month for 'AI Coach' subscription. Each new clip makes the coach smarter.
all_findings.recommended_action: Evaluate: b2c_product play

all_findings.title: Brand-skill split: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM users excel at kitchen (7.5), PSA users at power (6.29)
all_findings.evidence_summary: Among brands with 10+ clips: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM has highest avg kitchen mastery (7.5), while PSA has highest power rating (6.29).
all_findings.recommended_action: Build brand-skill correlation report for brand partnerships

all_findings.title: Facility benchmarking league — gamify venue quality
all_findings.evidence_summary: Rank facilities by player skill level, equipment quality, and content production. Facilities compete for 'Best Pickleball Venue' status. Drives Courtana installations.
all_findings.recommended_action: Evaluate: network_effect play

all_findings.title: Sponsorship whitespace detected in 3752 clips (96.7%) — sell to brands
all_findings.evidence_summary: We detect unbranded equipment/space in 3752 clips. This is the gap between what's visible and what's branded — sellable data.
all_findings.recommended_action: Package sponsorship whitespace data as 'brand opportunity report' for equipment companies

source_clips.summary: A 17-shot driving rally ends with an unforced error into the net.
Players engage in a prolonged kitchen dink rally, demonstrating patience and control.
Players engage in a consistent rally of dinks at the kitchen line.

all_findings.title: Social caption generation at 99.9% — ready to productize
all_findings.evidence_summary: Social media captions are generated for 3877/3879 clips. This is already a viable content-as-a-service product.
all_findings.recommended_action: Create API endpoint for social caption generation — charge per clip

all_findings.title: Skill standout: Ajr averages 8.5 in consistency across 6 clips
all_findings.evidence_summary: Ajr skill profile: consistency=8.5, kitchen=8.2, touch=8.0, composure=7.5, court_iq=7.0, court_coverage=6.2, athleticism=5.2, power=3.0, creativity=3.0
all_findings.recommended_action: Feature Ajr in coaching product showcase

source_clips.summary: A long kitchen rally ends with a powerful overhead smash.
A lengthy kitchen rally featuring consistent dinks ends with a net error.
Players engage in a lengthy kitchen rally, ending with a net error.

all_findings.title: Best predicted badges: stacking pro (F1=1.0), coverage duo (F1=1.0), airborne (F1=1.0)
all_findings.evidence_summary: Top performing badges by F1 score: stacking pro (F1=1.0), coverage duo (F1=1.0), airborne (F1=1.0)
all_findings.recommended_action: Use these badges as confidence anchors for the prediction system

all_findings.title: 'dink' players skew kitchen: power=3.6 vs kitchen=7.2
all_findings.evidence_summary: Players using dink as dominant shot (2415 clips) average power=3.6 and kitchen=7.2.
all_findings.recommended_action: Use shot type as player skill segmentation signal

all_findings.title: Emerging brands detected: 122 brands with 2-8 appearances
all_findings.evidence_summary: Rare but real brand detections: Dude Clothing (8), R8M (8), Gatorade (8), COACH (8), RECOVERY CARE (7), N/A (7), RECOVERY CAFE (7), Life Time (6). These represent market entrants or niche brands.
all_findings.recommended_action: Monitor emerging brand frequency over next 500 clips for trend confirmation

all_findings.title: Content creator partnership — pay top players for starring in viral clips
all_findings.evidence_summary: Our top 10 viral players are content gold. Sign them as 'Courtana Creators'. They get analytics + revenue share. We get consistent high-quality content.
all_findings.recommended_action: Evaluate: creator_economy play

all_findings.title: Viral rally length: top clips avg 16 shots vs bottom 11
all_findings.evidence_summary: Top-viral clips average 16.4 shots per rally, bottom-viral average 11.3.
all_findings.recommended_action: Investigate optimal rally length range for highlight reels

all_findings.title: Viral skill bias: kitchen = 7.3 in viral vs 4.5 in non-viral clips
all_findings.evidence_summary: Viral clips show kitchen average of 7.3 vs 4.5 in low-viral clips.
all_findings.recommended_action: Consider kitchen as a viral prediction feature

all_findings.title: Viral clips have more brand detections: 2.0 vs 1.1 per clip
all_findings.evidence_summary: Top-viral clips average 2.0 brand detections, bottom-viral average 1.1.
all_findings.recommended_action: Investigate if brand presence correlates with production quality or camera angle

all_findings.title: Player archetype distribution: Steady Eddie dominates (38/78 players)
all_findings.evidence_summary: Among 78 identified players: Steady Eddie: 38, Kitchen Master: 34, All-Rounder: 3, Dink Specialist: 3
all_findings.recommended_action: Build player DNA product with archetype classification as key feature

all_findings.title: Tier accuracy gap: 'wood' at 100% vs 'gold' at 97%
all_findings.evidence_summary: Badge tier prediction accuracy: wood=100%, brick=98%, silver=98%, bronze=97%, gold=97%
all_findings.recommended_action: Focus prompt improvement on gold-tier badges

all_findings.title: Arc-specific prompt issue: 'strategic' clips cause 94% of false positives
all_findings.evidence_summary: The 'strategic' arc type produces disproportionate badge false positives (15/16 = 94%). The prompt may be misinterpreting this arc type's visual patterns.
all_findings.recommended_action: Add arc-specific badge guidance: 'For strategic clips, badge X is common but Y is not'

all_findings.title: Coaching feedback covers 99.9% of clips — drill library is buildable now
all_findings.evidence_summary: Coaching analysis available for 3877/3879 clips.
all_findings.recommended_action: Build 'AI Coaching Library' — categorized drills from real game footage

all_findings.title: Prompt fix: Quality scores too clustered (avg=6.5, std=0.7) — increase spread
all_findings.evidence_summary: Quality scores cluster around 6.5 with std=0.7. Scores should use the full 1-10 range. Prompt may need calibration examples.
all_findings.recommended_action:
Add quality calibration to prompt:
'Quality score examples: 1-2 = unwatchable, 3-4 = poor angles/dark, 5-6 = standard gameplay, 7-8 = exciting with good camera, 9-10 = broadcast quality'

all_findings.title: Sell the highlight reel as NFT with embedded brand attribution
all_findings.evidence_summary: Each highlight clip becomes a collectible with brand logos baked into the metadata. Brands pay for attribution rights. Players earn from their own highlights.
all_findings.recommended_action: Evaluate: monetization_novel play
